thread_tweets.text: spoke to soon, they are still adding more 2m twaps
thread_tweets.full_text: spoke to soon, they are still adding more 2m twaps

thread_tweets.text:
1. Both, I am trading and holding. But I view it as a core position in my portfolio. I have even considered denominating in it (but haven't done so)  

When I have something I view as a core position there are a few approaches I take. Two separate accounts. One is the main position. One is for trading, swings or scalps. Early on I will take those wins, and then add those to the core position if its high conviction like this has been. As it progresses into the rally then the trading account portion just goes into cash and I no longer add to the core position. Later on, of course I look to sell. I will probably keep a good amount staked though bc I think the upside potential over several years is very high.

2. Hacks are always a risk. There is no way you can say that nothing is possible as much as we want to. But in general, no I'm not concerned about it. My main spot hype I dont keep on the exchange

3. Yes, I trade on HL as well as CB. I think it's a top tier product. And now that they are beginning to offer spot stocks I actually think I will likely even run some other strategies there that can't be run anywhere else

tweets.text:
Erase Israel from the equation. Erase whether Trump is good for Israel or bad for Israel. None of that matters.

If a country openly chants “Death to America” in its streets and parliament, develops ballistic missiles, moves closer and closer to nuclear weapons capability, and refuses to back down, then sooner or later America is going to have a problem with that country.

You can argue whether a war is smart, dumb, justified, unjustified, or worth the cost. That’s a separate discussion.

But pretending this is only about Israel misses the point. America has its own interests, its own security concerns, and its own red lines.

And as of now, it doesn’t look like Iran is giving up its nuclear stockpile or nuclear ambitions. If anything, it looks like it’s trying to do to Trump what it did to Obama: drag out negotiations, buy time, get sanctions relief-which now look like a lot better than what Obama gave them, get more money flowing into the country, and keep the program moving forward.
tweets.full_text:
Erase Israel from the equation. Erase whether Trump is good for Israel or bad for Israel. None of that matters.

If a country openly chants “Death to America” in its streets and parliament, develops ballistic missiles, moves closer and closer to nuclear weapons capability, and refuses to back down, then sooner or later America is going to have a problem with that country.

You can argue whether a war is smart, dumb, justified, unjustified, or worth the cost. That’s a separate discussion.

But pretending this is only about Israel misses the point. America has its own interests, its own security concerns, and its own red lines.

And as of now, it doesn’t look like Iran is giving up its nuclear stockpile or nuclear ambitions. If anything, it looks like it’s trying to do to Trump what it did to Obama: drag out negotiations, buy time, get sanctions relief-which now look like a lot better than what Obama gave them, get more money flowing into the country, and keep the program moving forward.

tweets.text:
Someone who doesn't have much can be more generous giving a little than a rich person giving a lot. Some people respond to the generosity while others respond to the money. You want the first type with you, and you always want to treat them generously.

When I had nothing, I was as generous as I could be with people who appreciated my generosity more than the higher levels of compensation others could afford to give them. For that reason, they stayed with me. I never forgot that, and I made a point of making them rich when I had the opportunity to do so. And they in turn were generous to me in their own way when I needed their generosity most. We both got something much more valuable than money--and we got the money too.

Remember that the only purpose of money is to get you what you want, so think hard about what you value and put it above money. How much would you sell a good relationship for? There's not enough money in the world to get you to part with a valued relationship. #principleoftheday

tweets.text:
The discrepancy is getting even larger, with STRC ticking sub $92 yesterday while SATA is trading at par.

SATA is 'only' paying 1.5% more per year, which shouldn't be enough to justify such a difference, I'm personally convinced that the market has no clue how to price these prefs correctly.

The nature of the holder base as well as the market cap of the pref does matter a lot. STRC increased from like $3bn mcap to $10bn mcap in 3 months, which implies that there is a LOT of rotations (from old/panicking to new holders) needed during each "depeg" to get back to par.

Strategy will increase their dividend rate to 12% in July, then probably even more in August. STRC will end up trading back to par, but it can take time. If BTC dumps further down, it will take EVEN more time.

But as long as STRC is trading below par, there are no inflows into BTC from STRC, so at least we can see BTC trade 'organically' without people trying to frontrun the STRC inflows.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
SATA has started the daily dividends and is now trading at par.

STRC is still at $95 because its market cap is so much larger (needs a lot more of hands changing), but if BTC has bottomed, it will get back to par too.

BTC not trading based on STRC flows is great. https://t.co/LOfnfKD5tc

tweets.text:
Something I’m wondering as I’m beginning to learn piano is how likely is it in the next 30 years that we could inject « skills » like « top piano players » via brain-computer interfaces similar to Neuralink? 

Like an artificial neuron (AI) enhancing my not artificial neurons? 

Some options:

- The device could detect I’m about to hit the wrong note and correct my motor intent before I even make the mistake.

- Not injecting skill but compressing the time it takes to learn. I could practice 10 minutes instead of 10 years. 

- The device modifies my synapses connections for me to become a top piano player right away 

There is a sort of nihilism associated with AGI where I’m always thinking: is that worth it or the model will help me do it in minutes instead of years soon? 🫠

tweets.text:
Congrats Genesis on the release of their first robot! Robotics is about to experience its ChatGPT moment. 

It’s refreshing to see new design patterns that don’t mimic humans. I like the absence of a proper head. I’m not sure I want to see a human-like shape robot in my home with my kid. 

I understand we need to copy some human features like the hand because it’s easier to collect training data but we should innovate elsewhere.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Humanoid robots don't need to look human.

Meet Eno, our first general-purpose robot.

Not a machine pretending to be human, but intelligence given a body.

At Genesis, we’re building a future where robots don’t feel cold or distant, but capable, calm, and ready to help. https://t.co/JisbMOcYzi
